results.rendered_html:
asamartha a.
1 Not able or competent.
2 Weak, feeble.
3 (In Rhet.) Powerless to convey the intended meaning, a defect of a word;
(pb) yattadarthaṃ paṭhyate na ca tatrāsya śāktiḥ tadasamarthatvaṃ  K. P. 7 ; e. g. in kuṃjaṃ haṃti kṛśodarī, han, though read in root-books in the sense of ‘going’, is here powerless to convey that meaning.
Comp.
— samāsaḥ a compound in which the conjunction of words is not as it should be; e. g. in aśrāddhabhojin, asūryaṃpaśya, a goes not with śrāddha or sūrya but with bhojin or paśya.
